title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Command Retrieval
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Text Inspection · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 4-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.

Which action demonstrates Text Inspection without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.

options.text: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures.
